The '''Naboo Swampspeeder''' was a two-seater aquatic speeder.

==Characteristics==
It had limited armor and considerable speed and maneuverability. It was equipped with two [[Light repeating blaster/Legends|light repeating blasters]] as well as two [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]], of similar firepower those found on the [[Gian heavy patrol speeder|V-19 landspeeder]]. Each [[Light repeating blaster/Legends|light repeating blaster]] and [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] were mounted very close to one another on either side of the fuselage, just in front of the cockpit, and were capable of training upward to attack foes above water level.

The [[Light repeating blaster/Legends|light repeating blasters]] fired in linked configuration, while the [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]] could be fired only one at a time, although they could be either fired rapidly or charged up for a powerful shot capable of destroying an [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tank]] in a single shot.

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SwampSpeeder1.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A side view of a Swamp Speeder]]

Oddly, no hangar description was recorded for the boat, making it the only unlockable vehicle in the game to not have a description voiced by Jeff Coopwood (as either [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]] or the game's narrator). The vehicle also doesn't appear in any stages naturally and no canon references yet exist outside the game to this boat, meaning no information on it is known besides its appearance and relative stats.

The vehicle was unlocked alongside the [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|AAT]] only after being awarded Platinum Medals for every level in the game, most likely the hardest challenge in any Star Wars-based [[Factor 5]] game. A password for unlocking the vehicle was later revealed as SWSPDR, and was most likely implemented due to worries that few - if any - players would ever see the vehicle.

Although its similarities to the [[Seraph-class urban landspeeder]] and [[V-19 landspeeder]] suggest the craft's manufacturer is [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]], no true evidence exists to the boat's origin. Even though it has been nicknamed the "Naboo Swamp Speeder" to avoid confusion with the [[Infantry Support Platform/Legends|Infantry Support Platform]] - also known as the "Swamp Speeder" - the prefix is so far merely a common misconception.
